qualification_scope.synthetic: Isolated incremental Gold Rush shadow-family LevelDB layout and I/O-shaped fixture operations, including one maximum-size 64-ID QQPROOFS bucket for every post-5,993,200 height. It excludes pre-existing and ordinary base UTXOs, ordinary or migration quantum outputs, production Coin and marker decoding/classification CPU, and terminal combined-chainstate effects.
qualification_scope.live: Current captured partial-mainnet combined chainstate using production blackcoind and getcirculatingsupply at the protected operator-attested tip. It does not project the terminal Gold Rush height.
qualification_scope.unproved: Terminal combined-chainstate physical amplification and full-cardinality production getcirculatingsupply wall time remain unproved until terminal-live evidence or a separately reviewed conservative background model exists.

production_release_authorization.remaining_gate: Final production publication requires terminal-live combined-chainstate and full-cardinality production getcirculatingsupply qualification, or a separately reviewed conservative equivalent.

live_partial_snapshot.scope: Protected-operator-attested current mainnet evidence proves real partial-epoch replay, immutable-snapshot provenance, lifecycle accounting, startup and current physical state under the v30.1.1 candidate. The offline verifier authenticates capture freshness and RPC/archive consistency but does not independently discover the global tip. The 5993200 QQP3 shadow boundary is prospective v30.1.1 behavior, not a v30.1.0 rule; base-block compatibility does not imply equal post-boundary shadow state. This evidence does not replace the exact synthetic completed-epoch maximum or claim that mainnet has reached height 6192999.

retention.rule: Compaction is required when exact synthetic steady or maximum-observed physical amplification, obsolete-file retention, or measured forced-compaction reclaim crosses its pinned budget. Periodic sampling is not asserted as an unseen transient upper bound. The release gate fails when compaction is required but no separately authenticated compaction protocol is implemented.
